question_text: A student measures a piece of metal with a mass of 540 g. When placed in a measuring cylinder containing 100 cm³ of water, the water level rises to 160 cm³. Calculate the density of the metal and explain whether it would float or sink in water (density of water = 1 g/cm³).
correct_answer: Density = 9 g/cm³; it would sink because 9 g/cm³ > 1 g/cm³
explanation: First, find the volume by subtracting: 160 - 100 = 60 cm³. Then use density = mass ÷ volume = 540 ÷ 60 = 9 g/cm³. Since the metal's density is much greater than water's density (1 g/cm³), it will sink.
